title: buddleja campestris
intro: Buddleja campestris, also known as the alpine or mountain butterfly bush, is a perennial shrub native to the mountainous regions of Asia, characterized by its clusters of small, fragrant flowers and silvery-green foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are believed to help alleviate symptoms of fever, coughs, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Buddleja campestris are thought to include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although the optimal methods and dosages for these preparations have not been fully established.
